Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19

Methodologically Supported Clinical Trial Early Sharing / Preprint ↗

Journal: New England Journal of Medicine
Publisher: Massachusetts Medical Society
Publication year: 2020
DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa2021436
Overview Story Impact Roles Access Mentions
More
Teaching Who's Citing Funding

The Story So Far

Short impact summary

The study titled "Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19" published in the New England Journal of Medicine has made a significant impact on the treatment of COVID-19, garnering over 10,910 citations. Supported by a robust array of funding agencies, including the National Institute for Health Research and the Medical Research Council, this research reflects strong institutional validation and community engagement.

Full narrative

The publication of "Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19" represents a pivotal moment in the context of the global health crisis caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. Not only has it achieved impressive citation metrics—over 10,910 citations as recorded in OpenAlex—but the work has also generated substantial public interest. Its societal relevance is underscored by mentions on platforms such as Wikipedia and Bluesky, indicating that the research has penetrated both academic and non-academic spheres.

The early dissemination of this research as a preprint, which has garnered 844 citations before its formal publication, showcases the authors' commitment to open science. This approach has fostered immediate community engagement and dialogue around the findings, illustrating the work's relevance and timeliness in addressing urgent clinical questions.

Moreover, this study benefits from robust financial support from esteemed funding agencies, including the National Institute for Health Research and the Wellcome Trust. Such backing not only validates the research effort but also enhances its credibility in the eyes of both the scientific community and the public.

Additionally, the research's active involvement in clinical trials, with an associated trial identifier, emphasizes its direct application and impact in patient care settings. The inclusion of Open Peer Reviews further strengthens the findings by enhancing the transparency and rigor of the review process, fostering a healthy discourse around its conclusions. Through its numerous citations and early engagement strategies, this work continues to play a crucial role in shaping treatment protocols and informing ongoing research in infectious diseases.
